title: leucas marrubioides var. pulneyensis
intro: Leucas marrubioides var. pulneyensis, also known as Pulney leucas, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the Western Ghats of India. It belongs to the family Lamiaceae and is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including the treatment of digestive disorders, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for skin conditions. In traditional medicine, Leucas marrubioides var. pulneyensis is prepared as a decoction, powder, or oil, and is also used in the preparation of herbal teas and infusions.
